{{Quote|Is that your Podracer, or are you selling scrap metal?|Navior|translated=1|audio=Navior-taunt.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
"'''Bullseye'''" '''Navior''' was a [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Navior's species|alien]] who [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|competed]] in [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]] throughout [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]] in [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. With a reputation as one of the more honest racers on the course, he [[Pilot/Legends|piloted]] a narrow [[Irateq]] [[RQ 550C Dart]] [[Podracer/Legends|Podracer]], a craft that was known for maneuvering around obstacles that would swiftly destroy larger Podracers. The Dart's burst of speed and agility discouraged the use of dirty tactics, such as sabotage or weapons, to gain an advantage. Navior performed well on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Aquilaris]], scoring record [[Time/Legends|times]] on the [[Sunken City/Legends|Sunken City]] racecourse.

==Biography==
{{Quote|I see 'Bullseye' Navior's just taken his place on the grid. That's a quick Podracer he's got there.|[[Troig/Legends|Troig]] race announcer [[Fodesinbeed Annodue/Legends|Fode Annodue]]|audio=Fode-Navoir.flac|<ref name="E1R" />}}
Navior was a [[Gender/Legends|male]] member of a [[Navior's species|wide-eyed humanoid]] [[Species/Legends|species]] who received the nickname "Bullseye"<ref name="E1R" /> due in part to the way his [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] were shaped and [[Color/Legends|colored]].<ref name="SWE" /> Navior competed as a professional [[Podracer (profession)/Legends|Podracer]]<ref name="E1R" /> during the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]],<ref name="NEC1">''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates the events of [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']] to the [[Standard year/Legends|year]] [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]]. The racing season presented in the [[Star Wars video games|game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]'' must have taken place during this year due to the appearances of [[Qui-Gon Jinn/Legends|Qui-Gon Jinn]], [[Jar Jar Binks/Legends|Jar Jar Binks]], and [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], which correspond to ''The Phantom Menace''.</ref> earning a reputation as one of the more honest [[Pilot/Legends|pilots]] on the track. However, despite having partaken in [[Podracing/Legends|Podraces]] across [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], Navior was still fairly obscure, even with his high-acceleration, traction-heavy [[Irateq]] [[RQ 550C Dart]] scoring numerous victories. Nevertheless, Navior gained a small but devoted cult following.<ref name="Prima">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]]</ref>

[[File:NaviorRacing.png|thumb|left|250px|"Bullseye" Navior (front) raced on Tatooine.]]
Eventually, Navior did come to be considered one of the best pilots in the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim Territories]]. The Irateq was acknowledged as one of the more versatile crafts in the sport, and with such a quality vehicle, Navior saw little point in cheating to get ahead.<ref name="Prima" /> The Irateq was notably fragile, however, and was prone to taking damage. As such, Navior had to rely heavily on his vehicle's handling to avoid any hefty collisions.<ref name="E1R" />

Navior and his Podracer achieved notable fame on the [[Water/Legends|watery]] Outer Rim [[Planet/Legends|world]] of [[Aquilaris]], where he scored numerous victories<ref name="E1R" /> and was known as a track favorite on many of the planet's courses,<ref name="SWE" /> including [[Sunken City/Legends|Sunken City]]. Navior excelled on the Sunken City track, which weaved through underwater tunnels and led into an old, rusted floating [[City/Legends|city]],<ref name="E1R" /> where Navior scored a record race [[Time/Legends|time]] of 05:43.010 [[Standard minute/Legends|minutes]]. Navior also held some fame on the planet [[Baroonda/Legends|Baroonda]], where he was the track favorite of a [[Baroonda drag course 1|drag course]].<ref name="E1R2">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'']] {{C|[[Wikipedia:Game Boy Color|Game Boy Color]] version}}</ref> Sometime before the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]] in 32 [[Galactic Standard Calendar|BBY]], Navior competed in a race on the [[Desert/Legends|desert]] planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] against fellow racers [[Jinn Reeso]], [[Boles Roor/Legends|Boles Roor]], [[Neva Kee/Legends|Neva Kee]], [[Ody Mandrell/Legends|Ody Mandrell]], and [[Clegg Holdfast/Legends|Clegg Holdfast]]. Navior was able to fly ahead of the whole pack and maintain the lead for a time.<ref name="Prima" /><ref name="Prima2">[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer: Prima's Official Strategy Guide|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''Racer'': Prima's Official Strategy Guide]] includes a picture of Navior, [[Jinn Reeso]], [[Boles Roor/Legends|Boles Roor]], [[Neva Kee/Legends|Neva Kee]], [[Ody Mandrell/Legends|Ody Mandrell]], and [[Clegg Holdfast/Legends|Clegg Holdfast]] competing in a [[Podracing/Legends|Podrace]] on [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. According to {{DB|character|nevakee|Kee, Neva}}, Kee disappeared during the the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]], which ''[[The New Essential Chronology]]'' dates to [[32 BBY/Legends|32 BBY]], never to be seen again. No source states that Navior or Jinn Reeso competed in the [[Boonta Eve Classic/Legends|Boonta Eve Classic]], so this article assumes that the pictured Tatooine race from the [[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|''Episode I Racer'']] strategy guide must have preceded the Boonta Eve race.</ref>

==Personality and traits==
[[File:BraveCage.png|thumb|right|200px|The Irateq RQ 550C Dart, "Bullseye" Navior's Podracer]]
"Bullseye" Navior was a brown-skinned [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]]<ref name="SWE" /> who had large, gaping, yellow eyes and brown–white [[Hair/Legends|fur]].<ref name="E1R" /> He was renowned by those who knew him as one of the few honest pilots on the course. Navior saw little point in cheating to gain the upper hand, as his vehicle was more than capable of getting ahead on its own. Navior's racing style attracted a cult following among Podracing fans.<ref name="Prima" />

==Equipment==
Navior piloted an Irateq RQ 550C Dart, a Podracer notably strong in handling, acceleration, and traction. However, the vehicle was not without its drawbacks, as it only had an average top speed and was very fragile. Although the Dart's [[Engine/Legends|engines]] sported a dull-gray color, the appearance of the craft looked largely unpainted. The front and back of the engines were red, while both engines had a pair of green fins situated on both the top and bottom. The wire-frame [[Cockpit/Legends|cockpit]] had two red antennae sticking from the top of it. When racing, Navior wore a [[Clothing/Legends|brown vest]] and lightly-colored pants, along with a pair of shin guards.<ref name="E1R" />

==Behind the scenes==
[[File:Navior_concept.jpg|thumb|left|125px|Episode I concept art by Terryl Whitlatch]]
"Bullseye" Navior was created as a concept Podracer pilot for the [[1999]] film [[Star Wars: Episode I The Phantom Menace|''Star Wars'': Episode I ''The Phantom Menace'']]. Conceptual artist [[Terryl Whitlatch]] designed the character during pre-production for inclusion in the film's Podracing sequence, but the character, along with several others, was cut from the film's final version.<ref name="Art">''[[The Art of Star Wars Episode I: The Phantom Menace]]''</ref> Navior's Irateq Podracer, however, made it as far as the film's animatic stages.<ref name="TPM">{{Film|I}} [[Star Wars home video releases|DVD special features]]</ref>

Navior, along with several other scrapped Podracer pilots, including [[Fud Sang/Legends|Fud Sang]], [[Slide Paramita]], and [[Toy Dampner]], were instead used for the tie-in [[Star Wars video games|video game]] ''[[Star Wars: Episode I Racer|Star Wars Episode I: Racer]]''. Navior is not available to players at the start of the game and must be unlocked by defeating Navior on his preferred course, Sunken City. Navior's final game design varies little from Whitlatch's original concept art, the biggest difference being the absence of Navior's [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]] in the game. Together with Podracer character [[Dud Bolt/Legends|Dud Bolt]], Navior was voiced by [[David Jeremiah]] in ''Episode I Racer''.<ref name="E1R" />
